axioms: A2 one local D1 profile and proposition value per site
A3 transport edges reference known sites
A4 patches reference known sites and closed finite variables
A5 D1 dimensions are nonnegative integers
A6 compatibility is checked locally before global-section claims
A7 morphisms preserve declared local dimensions and selected global obstructions
hypotheses: H0: informal graph metadata is sufficient
H1: finite graph-indexed D1 restriction system is sufficient
H2: presheaf or sheaf structure is already required
H3: categorical morphism structure is already required for IPT
H4: no canonical formalization is currently justified

evidence_against: morphism failure is only visible with explicit site maps
evidence_for: T25 could name the structure informally
hypothesis_evaluations.verdict: too weak for the theorem ladder

hypothesis_evaluations.claim: Finite graph-indexed D1 restriction system is sufficient.
evidence_against: not yet tested on continuous or covariant domains
evidence_for: scalar and vector projections are executable
transport and gluing failures are detected
positive and failed morphisms are distinguished

hypothesis_evaluations.claim: Presheaf or sheaf structure is already required.
evidence_against: finite patch constraints reproduce the tested obstruction
hypothesis_evaluations.verdict: defer full sheaf semantics until the finite object fails

hypothesis_evaluations.claim: Categorical morphism structure is already required for IPT.
evidence_against: current IPT objects lack site maps and restriction-map commutation data
hypothesis_evaluations.verdict: prepare IPT representation work, but do not claim it

hypothesis_evaluations.claim: No canonical formalization is currently justified.
evidence_against: one finite object reproduces the T25 theorem ladder
evidence_for: full sheaf and full IPT claims remain unearned
hypothesis_evaluations.verdict: too pessimistic for current finite evidence

missing_requirements: site maps on current IPT objects
ipt_readiness.verdict: T26 defines D1 restriction morphisms, but this is only a readiness step for IPT representation.

morphism_analyses.interpretation: declared local and global D1 restriction data are preserved
morphism_analyses.name: trusted_transport_relabeling

morphism_analyses.interpretation: morphism is blocked by an explicit preservation failure
morphism_analyses.name: connected_to_partitioned_transport

negative_results.boundary: scalar D1 remains valid as a local or recovered projection
negative_results.reflects: mathematical limit of scalar D1 as a global object
negative_results.what_failed: scalar projection loses observer distribution, graph data, or patch data outside uniform cases
negative_results.why_it_failed: componentwise scalar aggregation has no place to store site-indexed distinctions

negative_results.boundary: vector D1 remains valid for observer-distribution snapshots
negative_results.what_failed: identical vectors can differ in trusted reachability
negative_results.why_it_failed: connected_same_vector and partitioned_same_vector have the same vector but different transport

negative_results.boundary: full sheaf language remains optional, not disproved
negative_results.what_failed: local patch witnesses do not globalize
negative_results.why_it_failed: contextual_gluing_obstruction has local witnesses and zero global witnesses

negative_results.attempted_claim: current IPT formalism already has full D1 restriction representation
negative_results.boundary: T26 supplies a target morphism notion for a later IPT upgrade
negative_results.what_failed: T23 IPT objects lack site maps and restriction commutation data
negative_results.why_it_failed: T23 was designed for typed invariant transport, not local-to-global morphisms

recommendation: Adopt D1RestrictionSystem as the repo's next formal D1 extension. Keep scalar D1 as the fixed-site or uniform projection, keep vector D1 as the observer-distribution projection, use graph and patch data for transport and gluing claims, and defer full sheaf and full IPT representation claims until morphism composition is tested more broadly.

theorem_ladder.boundary: finite validation only; no continuous or Lorentz-covariant semantics claimed
theorem_ladder.name: Minimal Axiom Sufficiency Theorem
theorem_ladder.statement: The finite axioms validate every T25 scenario used by T26.

theorem_ladder.boundary: fails for divergent local profiles or nontrivial patch data
theorem_ladder.name: Scalar Recovery Theorem
theorem_ladder.statement: Scalar D1 is recovered when local profiles are uniform and no transport or gluing data is lost.

theorem_ladder.boundary: fails when reachability or patch compatibility matters
theorem_ladder.name: Vector Recovery Theorem
theorem_ladder.statement: Vector D1 is faithful when observer profiles diverge but graph and gluing data add no distinction.

theorem_ladder.boundary: does not require sheaf language by itself
theorem_ladder.name: Graph Necessity Theorem
theorem_ladder.statement: Graph transport data is necessary when identical vectors have different trusted reachability.

theorem_ladder.boundary: finite obstruction only; cohomological semantics remain optional
theorem_ladder.name: Gluing Obstruction Theorem
theorem_ladder.statement: Locally satisfiable D1 patches need not admit a global assignment.

theorem_ladder.boundary: composition laws and IPT representation are not proved here
theorem_ladder.name: Restriction Morphism Theorem
theorem_ladder.statement: A D1 restriction morphism is executable and can distinguish preserved transport from failed transport.

evidence: T26 morphisms are ready, but current T23 IPT objects are not
theorem_ladder.name: IPT Representation Theorem
theorem_ladder.statement: Full IPT representation requires IPTs to carry site maps and restriction-map commutation data.
